message:
test(core): property-based tests for crash recovery module (wa-he2a)

39 proptest properties covering HealthSnapshot, ShutdownSummary, CrashReport,
CrashManifest, CrashLoopDetector state machine (backoff monotonicity, cap,
threshold triggers, window pruning, diagnostics consistency), CaptureCheckpoint
skip logic, IncidentKind/ReplayMode enums, and RedactionReport consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -680,7 +680,9 @@
 {"id":"wa-7o4f","title":"Mux notifications: coalesce PaneOutput and run subscribers outside lock","description":"## Goal
Reduce contention and notification storms in the vendored WezTerm mux notification path by:
1) ensuring subscriber callbacks are never executed while holding the global `subscribers` lock, and
2) coalescing high-frequency `PaneOutput` notifications into bounded batches.

## Background
Current behavior (vendored mux):
- `send_actions_to_mux` applies actions then calls `Mux::notify_from_any_thread(MuxNotification::PaneOutput(..))`.
- `Mux::notify` takes a write lock on `subscribers` and executes callbacks inside `retain(...)`.
- Under bursty output (agents, `cat /dev/urandom`), this can amplify work: per-batch main-thread spawns + global lock hold + callback fanout.

## Proposed design (language-neutral spec)
### A) Callback-outside-lock fanout
- Snapshot the subscriber list under lock (clone IDs + function pointers/Arcs).
- Drop the lock.
- Invoke callbacks.
- Reacquire lock only to remove subscribers that returned false.

### B) Coalesced output notifications
- Maintain a per-pane `pending_output` flag and a coalescing wakeup to the main thread.
- Producers set `pending_output=true` and only schedule a main-thread wakeup once per “tick”.
- The main thread drains all panes with `pending_output=true`, clears them, and emits `PaneOutput` notifications (either as a batch or one-by-one, but without per-event spawning).

### C) Backpressure + safety
- Use bounded queues for cross-thread notification transport.
- Define policy for overflow: coalesce/merge (preferred) rather than unbounded growth.

## Acceptance criteria
- Semantics preserved: subscribers can still unsubscribe (return false), ordering constraints documented.
- No callback execution under `subscribers` lock.
- `PaneOutput` notification path is measurably lower overhead under stress (add a micro-bench or histogram).
- No unsafe code.

## Files (expected)
- `frankenterm/mux/src/lib.rs` (MuxNotification fanout; notify_from_any_thread)
- Possibly `frankenterm/promise` (if needed for coalesced main-thread scheduling)

## Cross-references
- `wa-x4rq` (coalescing in ft runtime) — align coalescing semantics.
- `evidence/ghostty/event-system.md` (wa-3bja.4) — Ghostty’s coalesced wakeups + mailbox drain patterns.

## CRITICAL: /porting-to-rust skill requirement
Any implementation work on this bead MUST use `/porting-to-rust` and follow a language-neutral spec-first workflow.
","notes":"Implemented in frankenterm/mux/src/lib.rs: (1) subscriber fanout now snapshots callbacks and executes outside subscribers lock, then prunes false-returning subscribers; (2) PaneOutput notify_from_any_thread path now coalesces per-pane notifications via pending_output_notifications + single scheduled main-thread drain; (3) added histograms mux.notify.subscriber_fanout and mux.notify.pane_output_batch_size; (4) added unit tests notify_allows_subscribe_during_callback and pane_output_drain_coalesces_duplicate_pane_ids. Validation: OPENSSL_NO_VENDOR=1 cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo fmt --check passed; OPENSSL_NO_VENDOR=1 cargo test -p mux [new tests] passed. Workspace clippy -D warnings fails on broad pre-existing unrelated lint debt outside mux/lib.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:08:38.66749-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:18:30.917424Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:18:30.917375Z"}

 {"id":"wa-7wk","title":"Decision context capture: PolicyEngine records full reasoning for every decision","description":"# Task: Decision Context Capture

## Goal
Modify PolicyEngine and workflow runner to capture **full decision context** (not just the result) for every policy decision.

## Why This Matters
To explain "why" a decision was made, we need to know:
- What conditions were evaluated
- Which rules matched or did not match
- What evidence was considered
- How the final decision was reached

Without this context, `wa why` cannot provide meaningful explanations.

## Current State
PolicyEngine returns `PolicyDecision`:
```rust
enum PolicyDecision {
    Allow,
    Deny { reason: String },
    RequireApproval { code: String },
}
```

This is insufficient for explanation—we know the result but not the reasoning.

## Target State
PolicyEngine returns `PolicyDecision` with full context:
```rust
struct PolicyDecision {
    result: PolicyResult,
    context: DecisionContext,
}

struct DecisionContext {
    /// Timestamp of decision
    timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    
    /// Action being evaluated
    action: ActionKind,
    
    /// Target of action
    target: ActionTarget,
    
    /// Capabilities observed
    capabilities: PaneCapabilities,
    
    /// Rules evaluated, in order
    rules_evaluated: Vec<RuleEvaluation>,
    
    /// The rule that determined the outcome (if any)
    determining_rule: Option<RuleId>,
    
    /// Evidence collected during evaluation
    evidence: Vec<Evidence>,
    
    /// Rate limit state at decision time
    rate_limit_state: Option<RateLimitState>,
}

struct RuleEvaluation {
    rule_id: RuleId,
    matched: bool,
    reason: Option<String>,
}

enum Evidence {
    PaneState { key: String, value: String },
    Timestamp { key: String, value: DateTime<Utc> },
    Flag { key: String, value: bool },
}
```

## Implementation

### 1. Capture During Evaluation
```rust
impl PolicyEngine {
    pub fn authorize(&self, action: &Action) -> PolicyDecision {
        let mut context = DecisionContext::new(action);
        
        // Capture capabilities
        context.capabilities = self.derive_capabilities(action.pane_id);
        
        // Evaluate rules, recording each
        for rule in &self.rules {
            let evaluation = rule.evaluate(&context.capabilities);
            context.rules_evaluated.push(evaluation.clone());
            
            if evaluation.matched && evaluation.is_terminal() {
                context.determining_rule = Some(rule.id.clone());
                break;
            }
        }
        
        // Build decision with context
        PolicyDecision {
            result: self.compute_result(&context),
            context,
        }
    }
}
```

### 2. Store in Audit Trail
```sql
-- Extend audit_actions table
ALTER TABLE audit_actions ADD COLUMN decision_context BLOB;
-- Store as MessagePack for efficiency, or JSON for debuggability
```

### 3. Query Interface
```rust
impl StorageHandle {
    pub fn get_decision_context(
        &self,
        decision_id: &str,
    ) -> Result<DecisionContext, Error>;
    
    pub fn get_recent_decisions(
        &self,
        filter: DecisionFilter,
        limit: usize,
    ) -> Result<Vec<(Decision, DecisionContext)>, Error>;
}
```

## Storage Considerations
- Context can be large; consider compression
- Retention policy: keep context for N days, then prune
- Index by pane_id, action_type for efficient queries

## Testing
- Unit tests: Context is captured correctly for various scenarios
- Integration tests: Context survives storage roundtrip
- Query tests: Filtering by pane/action works correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every PolicyDecision includes full DecisionContext
- Context is persisted in audit trail
- Context can be queried by decision_id, pane_id, action_type
- Context is sufficient for `wa why` to generate explanations

 {"id":"wa-809y","title":"Workflow audit integration: record per-step actions/outcomes into audit trail","description":"# Task: Workflow audit integration

## Goal
Ensure workflows are explainable and trustworthy by recording an audit trail for every workflow action attempt.

Workflows can:
- send text
- issue control sequences (Ctrl-C)
- wait for conditions
- perform account/device-auth steps (later)

Users need to know exactly what wa did and why.

## Requirements
- For each workflow execution:
  - record an audit entry for each step action attempt (even if denied or failed)
  - include `step_id` / `step_name` / `workflow_name`
  - include policy decision and preconditions observed
  - include verification outcomes (wait-for matched, timeout)

- Redaction:
  - never store raw secrets or full inputs
  - store hashes/summaries consistent with the redaction system

- Correlation:
  - link audit entries to `workflow_execution_id`
  - link to the originating `event_id` when available

## Testing
- Integration tests:
  - run a synthetic workflow that attempts a send and a wait
  - verify audit timeline entries exist for both attempts and correlate to the workflow execution
- Redaction tests:
  - ensure the audit record never contains raw inputs/pane tails

## Acceptance Criteria
- A workflow run produces a coherent audit timeline that matches the workflow_step_log.
- Failures still produce audit entries that explain what was attempted and what was denied.

 {"id":"wa-h5w9","title":"E2E: pane_uuid remains stable across rename/move","description":"# Task: E2E pane_uuid stability

## Goal
Prove in a real WezTerm session that pane_uuid is stable across common churn.

## Scenario
- Create a pane.
- Ensure pane_uuid assigned.
- Rename pane/title and/or move between tabs.
- Verify:
  - same pane_uuid continues
  - wa continues to attribute output/events correctly

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps; use wait-for/quiescence.
- Artifacts include:
  - initial pane state
  - post-churn pane state
  - logs showing identity continuity

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E deterministically validates identity stability.

 {"id":"wa-hfis","title":"Robot mode tests: envelope stability, error codes, command outputs (synthetic)","description":"# Task: Robot mode tests

## Goal
Prevent breaking changes in robot schemas and error codes.

Robot mode is the primary API surface for other tools/agents. A minor formatting change can break automation.

## Coverage

### Envelope stability
- Assert the JSON envelope contains the expected top-level fields (and only intentional additions):
  - ok
  - data (on success)
  - error (on failure)
  - hint (optional)
  - elapsed_ms
  - version
  - now

### Error code stability
- For common error cases, assert:
  - error.code is stable
  - error.message is actionable
  - hint is present when it improves remediation

### Command-level contract tests (incremental)
- **Phase 1 robot commands:**
  - state
  - get-text
  - search
  - events
  - send
  - wait-for

- **Harmonized commands (wa-d2z6):**
  - why (wa-zcvm - implemented)
  - approve (wa-43rm)
  - workflow run/list/status/abort (wa-n8cd tests these)

- **Later phases:**
  - accounts / accounts-refresh
  - rules list/test
  - reservations / reserve / release

## Schema approach
- Validate outputs against versioned JSON schemas (bd-4vx.7.10) in addition to snapshots.
- Snapshots are still useful for human review (especially for error payloads), but schema validation is the real guardrail.

## Testing Requirements

### Robot Why Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_robot_why_known_code() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["why", "deny.alt_screen"]);
    assert!(output["ok"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert!(!output["data"]["explanation"].as_str().unwrap().is_empty());
}

#[test]
fn test_robot_why_unknown_code() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["why", "unknown.code"]);
    assert!(!output["ok"].as_bool().unwrap());
    // Should include list of valid codes
    assert!(output["hint"].as_str().unwrap().contains("available codes"));
}
```

### Robot Approve Tests
See wa-43rm for comprehensive approve testing.

### Workflow Subcommand Tests
See wa-n8cd for comprehensive workflow testing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests fail if:
  - a required field is removed/renamed
  - an error code changes
  - output becomes non-JSON or non-deterministic

## Meta-validation
- Include a "schema + snapshot mismatch" test to ensure both systems are actually running (not silently skipped).
- Add at least one test per command that asserts deterministic field ordering (or explicitly documents if ordering is not guaranteed).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:00:05.210707736Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.293553-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-29T07:00:51.026827639Z"}
 {"id":"wa-hgk4","title":"E2E: large transcript search remains fast (verbose perf artifacts)","description":"# Task: E2E large transcript search performance

## Goal
Provide an end-to-end scenario that exercises storage + indexing + search under realistic scale.

## Requirements
- Generate or replay a large transcript (prefer fixtures/synthetic to avoid nondeterminism).
- Run representative queries:
  - short term
  - multi-word
  - scoped by pane/time
- Capture perf artifacts:
  - elapsed times
  - index lag signals
  - DB size snapshot

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E produces stable metrics and flags regressions clearly.

 {"id":"wa-hj458","title":"Phase 1: asupersync foundation and workspace integration","description":"# Phase 1: Add asupersync to workspace

## Goal
Add asupersync as a workspace dependency and establish the project-level migration infrastructure. This bead is ONLY about getting asupersync compiling in the workspace — the Cx pattern, Outcome strategy, and compat layer are separate beads.

## Key decisions
1. **Dependency method**: Add asupersync as a git dependency (pinned to a specific commit) or crates.io dependency in workspace Cargo.toml
2. **MSRV check**: asupersync requires Rust 1.75+ — verify our toolchain supports this
3. **Compilation check**: Ensure asupersync compiles cleanly on macOS (kqueue reactor) alongside existing tokio

## Deliverables
- asupersync + asupersync-macros added to workspace Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]
- wa-core/Cargo.toml gains optional dependency on asupersync
- Feature flag 'asupersync-runtime' defined (default off)
- cargo build succeeds with both default features and --features asupersync-runtime
- cargo check --all-targets passes

## What this does NOT include (tracked separately)
- Cx infrastructure (wa-2lp7o)
- Outcome strategy (wa-d0m4t)
- Runtime compat layer (wa-1j0ye)

## Acceptance criteria
- asupersync compiles as part of the workspace
- Feature flag exists and both paths build
- No existing tests broken by adding the dependency

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks**: Measure Cx creation overhead — target <50ns per Cx instantiation. Add `benches/cx_creation.rs` using criterion with `criterion_group!` and `criterion_main!` macros. Benchmark both `current_thread()` and `multi_thread()` RuntimeBuilder presets.

## Cross-references
- See wa-brc7d (asupersync FrankenTerm crates epic) for the broader crate migration plan that this foundation enables.","notes":"Done: added `asupersync-macros` to root [workspace.dependencies]; defined `asupersync-runtime` feature in `frankenterm-core` + `frankenterm` (default off); added `crates/frankenterm-core/benches/cx_creation.rs` gated by `asupersync-runtime`.

Fixes: `frankenterm-core` was not compiling due to missing `differential_snapshot` module; added minimal `src/differential_snapshot.rs` + cleaned test-only imports. Updated spectral bench + proptests to match `classify_signal` + new `detect_peaks` signature + median definition.

Verified: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo build`, `cargo build -p frankenterm --features asupersync-runtime`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets --features asupersync-runtime`, `cargo test -p frankenterm-core`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:47:45.04664Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:42:13.264875-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:42:13.264881-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-hj458","depends_on_id":"wa-e34d9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:04:24.30603-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}

 {"id":"wa-wo94","title":"Profile format + storage layout","description":"## What
Define how profiles are stored on disk and referenced.

## Why
A predictable layout is required for tooling and safe updates.

## How
- Decide path: `~/.config/wa/profiles/<name>.toml`
- Define profile metadata (description, last_updated)
- Ensure default profile remains implicit

## Success Criteria

 {"id":"wa-wqpd","title":"Renderers + TUI/web: show annotations","description":"## What
Update renderers and UI surfaces to display annotations and triage state.

## Why
Annotations are useless if not visible or filterable in common views.

## How
- CLI renderers include note/label/state columns in verbose mode
- TUI: detail view shows notes/labels and triage actions
- Web: read-only annotation display and filters

## Success Criteria
- Rendering stays concise in non-verbose output
- Filters by label/state work in UI","notes":"Implemented annotation/triage rendering surfaces across CLI/TUI/web. CLI: wa events --verbose now appends an annotation table (event/triage/labels/note) while keeping default concise output unchanged. TUI: event query now loads annotations; Events detail panel displays triage state, labels, and note. Web: /events now includes optional annotations object (triage_state, note, labels) and retains triage/label filters.  {"id":"wa-wvw7","title":"[EPIC] Search UX improvements (linting + maintenance)","description":"## Background
Search is core to wa, but users struggle with syntax errors and lack a way to repair/rebuild indexes when FTS gets stale.

## Goals
- Provide query linting + suggestions for common mistakes
- Add search autocomplete/suggestions in CLI/TUI
- Add FTS maintenance commands (verify/rebuild) with progress

## Non-Goals
- New search engine; stick to existing FTS5

## Considerations
- Must not leak sensitive content in lint output
- Maintenance commands should be safe and idempotent

## Success Criteria
- Users get actionable errors for invalid queries
- FTS maintenance commands run deterministically
- Unit + e2e tests cover linting and rebuild","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:09:20.853144146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.263287-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:48.049780253Z","close_reason":"Merged into canonical wa-upg.11 lane (FTS explainability + search UX maintenance); child tasks completed.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-wvw7","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-wyr1","title":"Design WaEventSink trait and IPC protocol for native WezTerm integration","description":"## Overview

Design the interface between vendored WezTerm and wa. This defines the contract for how WezTerm will emit events to wa without Lua involvement.

## Deliverables

### 1. WaEventSink Trait Definition

\`\`\`rust
/// Trait for receiving events from WezTerm.
/// 
/// Implementations should be non-blocking and thread-safe.
/// WezTerm calls these methods from various threads.
pub trait WaEventSink: Send + Sync + 'static {
    /// Called when new output is received for a pane.
    /// 
    /// # Arguments
    /// * \`pane_id\` - The WezTerm pane ID
    /// * \`data\` - Raw bytes of terminal output
    fn on_pane_output(&self, pane_id: u64, data: &[u8]);
    
    /// Called when pane state changes (title, dimensions, alt-screen).
    /// 
    /// # Arguments
    /// * \`pane_id\` - The WezTerm pane ID
    /// * \`state\` - New state snapshot
    fn on_pane_state_change(&self, pane_id: u64, state: &WaPaneState);
    
    /// Called when a user-var (OSC 1337) is set.
    /// 
    /// # Arguments
    /// * \`pane_id\` - The WezTerm pane ID
    /// * \`name\` - Variable name
    /// * \`value\` - Variable value
    fn on_user_var_changed(&self, pane_id: u64, name: &str, value: &str);
    
    /// Called when a new pane is created.
    fn on_pane_created(&self, pane_id: u64, domain: &str, cwd: Option<&str>);
    
    /// Called when a pane is destroyed.
    fn on_pane_destroyed(&self, pane_id: u64);
}

/// Pane state snapshot for state change events.
pub struct WaPaneState {
    pub title: String,
    pub rows: u16,
    pub cols: u16,
    pub is_alt_screen: bool,
    pub cursor_row: u32,
    pub cursor_col: u32,
}
\`\`\`

### 2. IPC Protocol Design

Protocol for WezTerm→wa communication over Unix socket:

**Message Format**: JSON Lines (newline-delimited JSON)

\`\`\`json
{"type":"pane_output","pane_id":0,"data":"base64...","ts":1706123456789}
{"type":"state_change","pane_id":0,"state":{"title":"zsh","rows":24,"cols":80,"is_alt_screen":false},"ts":...}
{"type":"user_var","pane_id":0,"name":"wa-ready","value":"1","ts":...}
{"type":"pane_created","pane_id":1,"domain":"local","cwd":"/home/user","ts":...}
{"type":"pane_destroyed","pane_id":1,"ts":...}
\`\`\`

**Socket Location**: \`$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/wa/events.sock\` or \`/tmp/wa-$USER/events.sock\`

**Connection Model**: 
- WezTerm connects to wa socket on startup (if available)
- Reconnects with backoff if connection lost
- Events dropped silently if wa not running (non-blocking)

### 3. Configuration

WezTerm config addition:
\`\`\`lua
-- Optional: enable wa integration
config.wa_event_socket = "/tmp/wa-user/events.sock"
config.wa_event_filter = {
    pane_output = true,      -- can be high volume
    state_change = true,
    user_var = true,
    pane_lifecycle = true,
}
\`\`\`

Or via environment variable:
\`\`\`bash
export WEZTERM_WA_SOCKET="/tmp/wa/events.sock"
\`\`\`

### 4. Thread Safety Considerations

WezTerm is heavily multi-threaded:
- Terminal parsing happens on dedicated threads
- UI updates on main thread
- User-var parsing on PTY reader threads

WaEventSink must be \`Send + Sync\`:
- Use \`Arc<WaEventSink>\` for shared ownership
- Internal buffering with lock-free queues if needed
- Non-blocking sends (drop events rather than block)

### 5. Rate Limiting (Optional)

Consider rate limiting high-frequency events:
- pane_output: Could batch/throttle to reduce IPC overhead
- state_change: Already rate-limited by WezTerm's internal update frequency

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] WaEventSink trait defined with doc comments
- [ ] IPC protocol documented (message types, format)
- [ ] Socket location strategy defined
- [ ] Configuration options specified
- [ ] Thread safety requirements documented
- [ ] Rate limiting strategy decided
- [ ] Design reviewed by maintainer

## Files to Create

- docs/vendored-wezterm-design.md — comprehensive design doc
- crates/wa-core/src/wezterm_native.rs — trait definitions (stub)

## References

- WezTerm mux architecture: https://github.com/wez/wezterm/tree/main/mux
- Unix socket IPC: tokio UnixStream
- JSON Lines: https://jsonlines.org/","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:41.716149348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.281818-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-30T23:09:15.471993056Z","close_reason":"Added vendored WezTerm native event design doc + WaEventSink trait stub","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-wyr1","depends_on_id":"wa-bjm9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-z0e.4","title":"Recording CLI commands: wa record start/stop/list, wa replay","description":"# Recording CLI commands

## Purpose
Provide user-friendly CLI commands for recording and replaying terminal sessions.

## Commands

### wa record start
```bash
wa record start [--pane <ID>] [--title "Session name"] [--auto-stop <duration>]

# Examples
wa record start --pane 0 --title "Debugging usage limit"
wa record start --pane 0 --auto-stop 30m
```

Output:
```
Recording started for pane 0
Recording ID: rec-2026-01-18-001
Press Ctrl+C or run `wa record stop` to end
```

### wa record stop
```bash
wa record stop [--pane <ID>] [--all]

# Examples
wa record stop --pane 0
wa record stop --all
```

Output:
```
Recording stopped
Duration: 5m 23s
Frames: 1,234
Size: 256 KB (compressed from 1.2 MB)
Saved: ~/.local/share/wa/recordings/rec-2026-01-18-001.war
```

### wa record list
```bash
wa record list [--limit <N>] [--format json|table]

# Output
ID                      Duration  Size    Pane  Title
rec-2026-01-18-001      5m 23s    256KB   0     Debugging usage limit
rec-2026-01-17-003      12m 45s   512KB   1     Codex workflow test
```

### wa replay
```bash
wa replay <recording-id-or-path> [options]

Options:
  --speed <0.5|1|2|4>     Playback speed (default: 1)
  --from <timestamp>      Start from timestamp (e.g., "1m30s")
  --to <timestamp>        Stop at timestamp
  --events-only          Show only event annotations
  --tui                  Use TUI player with controls

# Examples
wa replay rec-2026-01-18-001
wa replay rec-2026-01-18-001 --speed 2 --from 2m
wa replay ./my-recording.war --tui
```

### wa record info
```bash
wa record info <recording-id-or-path>

# Output
Recording: rec-2026-01-18-001
Title: Debugging usage limit
Created: 2026-01-18 10:15:00
Duration: 5m 23s
Frames: 1,234
Size: 256 KB
Compression: 78%
Pane: 0 (zsh @ /home/user/project)
Agent: Codex
Events: 3 (compaction, usage_warning, usage_limit)
```

## Robot Mode
```bash
wa robot record start --pane 0
wa robot record stop
wa robot record list
wa robot replay <id> --headless --output events.jsonl
```

## Testing
- CLI argument parsing tests
- Integration tests for record/stop/replay flow
- Output format tests (table, JSON)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] wa record start works with all options
- [ ] wa record stop gracefully ends recording
- [ ] wa record list shows recordings with metadata
- [ ] wa replay plays back accurately
- [ ] wa record info shows detailed metadata
- [ ] Robot mode commands produce valid JSON
- [ ] Tests cover all commands","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:49:30.44987641Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-07T02:21:13.537495408Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T02:21:13.537368733Z"}
 {"id":"wa-z0e.5","title":"Recording export: HTML player, privacy redaction, sharing","description":"# Recording export and sharing

## Purpose
Enable users to share recordings with others, including an HTML player for those without wa installed, and privacy controls for sensitive content.

## Export Formats

### Standalone HTML Player
```bash
wa record export <id> --format html --output recording.html

# Creates self-contained HTML file with embedded player
# Uses asciinema-player or custom player
# No server required - opens in any browser
```

HTML player features:
- Terminal-accurate rendering
- Speed control (0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x)
- Pause/seek/play
- Event annotations as clickable markers
- Responsive design

### Asciinema Format
```bash
wa record export <id> --format asciinema --output recording.cast

# Compatible with asciinema.org and asciinema-player
```

### GIF Export
```bash
wa record export <id> --format gif --output recording.gif

# For documentation and quick sharing
# Configurable size, frame rate, quality
```

## Privacy Redaction

### Redaction Rules
```rust
pub struct RedactionConfig {
    patterns: Vec<RedactionPattern>,
    mode: RedactionMode,
}

pub enum RedactionMode {
    Mask,       // Replace with ***
    Remove,     // Delete entirely
    Placeholder(String), // Replace with custom text
}

pub struct RedactionPattern {
    pattern: Regex,
    description: String,  // "API keys", "passwords"
}
```

### Built-in Patterns
- API keys (sk-*, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, etc.)
- Passwords (password=*, --password)
- Tokens (Bearer *, token=*)
- Personal info (email patterns, IP addresses)

### Usage
```bash
wa record export <id> --redact-secrets --output safe-recording.war
wa record export <id> --redact-pattern "CUSTOM_SECRET_\w+" --output safe.war
```

## Sharing Workflow
```bash
# Export with redaction
wa record export rec-001 --redact-secrets --format html --output debug-session.html

# Share the file
# User opens in browser - no wa installation needed
```

## Testing
- HTML player renders correctly in major browsers
- Redaction removes sensitive content
- Export/import roundtrip preserves non-redacted content
- GIF export produces valid files

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HTML export creates self-contained player
- [ ] HTML player works in Chrome, Firefox, Safari
- [ ] Asciinema format compatible with player
- [ ] GIF export produces reasonable quality
- [ ] Redaction removes secrets from output
- [ ] Custom redaction patterns work
- [ ] Tests verify redaction completeness","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:49:46.67099695Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-07T02:06:56.007398147Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T02:06:56.007272684Z"}
 {"id":"wa-z0e.6","title":"Recording tests: format parsing, roundtrip, playback accuracy, fuzz","description":"# Recording tests

## Purpose
Comprehensive test coverage for the recording system to ensure reliability and prevent regressions.

## Test Categories

### 1. Format Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn war_header_roundtrip() {
    let header = WarHeader::new(/* ... */);
    let bytes = header.to_bytes();
    let parsed = WarHeader::from_bytes(&bytes).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(header, parsed);
}

#[test]
fn war_rejects_invalid_magic() {
    let bad_bytes = b"NOT_WAR\x01...";
    assert!(WarHeader::from_bytes(bad_bytes).is_err());
}
```

### 2. Delta Encoding Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn delta_encoding_correctness() {
    let frame1 = b"Hello, world!";
    let frame2 = b"Hello, Rust!";
    
    let delta = DeltaEncoding::diff(frame1, frame2);
    let reconstructed = delta.apply(frame1);
    
    assert_eq!(&reconstructed, frame2);
}

#[proptest]
fn delta_roundtrip(original: Vec<u8>, modified: Vec<u8>) {
    let delta = DeltaEncoding::diff(&original, &modified);
    let reconstructed = delta.apply(&original);
    prop_assert_eq!(reconstructed, modified);
}
```

### 3. Record/Replay Roundtrip
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn record_replay_roundtrip() {
    let recorder = Recorder::new(tempfile());
    recorder.start();
    
    // Simulate output
    recorder.on_output_delta(&OutputDelta::new(b"Line 1\n")).await;
    recorder.on_output_delta(&OutputDelta::new(b"Line 2\n")).await;
    recorder.stop();
    
    // Replay
    let mut captured = Vec::new();
    let mut player = Player::new(recorder.path(), HeadlessSink::new(&mut captured));
    player.play().await;
    
    assert_eq!(captured, b"Line 1\nLine 2\n");
}
```

### 4. Playback Timing
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn playback_timing_accuracy() {
    let recording = create_test_recording(vec![
        (0, "frame1"),
        (1000, "frame2"),  // 1 second later
        (2000, "frame3"),  // 2 seconds later
    ]);
    
    let start = Instant::now();
    let mut player = Player::new(&recording, HeadlessSink::new(&mut vec![]));
    player.play().await;
    let elapsed = start.elapsed();
    
    // Should take ~2 seconds (within tolerance)
    assert!(elapsed >= Duration::from_millis(1900));
    assert!(elapsed <= Duration::from_millis(2100));
}
```

### 5. Seeking Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn seek_to_keyframe() {
    let recording = create_test_recording_with_keyframes();
    let mut player = Player::new(&recording, HeadlessSink::new(&mut vec![]));
    
    player.seek_to(5000).unwrap();  // 5 seconds
    assert!(player.position.timestamp_ms >= 4900);
    assert!(player.position.timestamp_ms <= 5100);
}
```

### 6. Fuzz Tests
```rust
#[fuzz]
fn fuzz_war_parser(data: &[u8]) {
    // Should not panic on arbitrary input
    let _ = Recording::from_bytes(data);
}

#[fuzz]
fn fuzz_delta_decoder(data: &[u8]) {
    let _ = DeltaEncoding::from_bytes(data);
}
```

### 7. E2E Tests
```bash
# Full record/replay cycle
./scripts/e2e_recording.sh --scenario basic
./scripts/e2e_recording.sh --scenario with-events
./scripts/e2e_recording.sh --scenario export-html
```

## Coverage Requirements
- Format parsing: 100%
- Delta encoding: property-based coverage
- Playback: timing within 5% tolerance
- Seeking: all keyframe scenarios
- Export: all formats

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Format roundtrip tests pass
- [ ] Delta encoding property tests pass
- [ ] Record/replay roundtrip works
- [ ] Timing accuracy within tolerance
- [ ] Seeking tests pass
- [ ] Fuzz tests find no panics
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass

## Testing
- Roundtrip tests for recording -> playback -> transcript equivalence.
- Fuzz tests for format parser stability.
- E2E: replay a recording and verify deterministic output.

## E2E logging requirements
- Recording E2E scripts must log:
  - recording start/stop timestamps
  - frame counts and sizes
  - playback speed used
  - checksum/verification of replayed output
- Artifacts must include the recorded file(s), replay transcript, and a summary JSON.

 {"id":"wa-zcvm","title":"Add wa robot why command","description":"## Goal
Add `wa robot why <code>` command for agents to understand error codes and policy denials.

## Rationale
When agents receive errors like `robot.policy_denied` or `robot.require_approval`, they need programmatic access to explanations and remediation steps.

## Implementation
1. Add `RobotCommands::Why { code: String }`
2. Query `wa_core::explanations::get_explanation(code)`
3. Return JSON with: scenario, brief, detailed, suggestions, see_also
4. Create wa-robot-why.json schema

## Response Format
\`\`\`json
{
  "ok": true,
  "data": {
    "code": "deny.alt_screen",
    "scenario": "Send denied because alt-screen is active",
    "brief": "Pane is in full-screen mode (vim, less, etc.)",
    "detailed": "The pane is currently displaying...",
    "suggestions": ["Exit the full-screen application first", ...],
    "see_also": ["wa policy", "wa status --pane <id>"]
  }
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa robot why deny.alt_screen` returns explanation
- `wa robot why unknown.code` returns error with available codes
- JSON schema validates response","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:14.633050084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.250398-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:04.476841497Z","close_reason":"Implemented: wa robot why command using get_explanation()","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-zcvm","depends_on_id":"wa-d2z6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-zehp","title":"Implement read-only endpoints: /panes /events /search (shared query layer)","description":"# Task: Implement read-only web endpoints

## Goal
Expose minimal read-only endpoints for dashboards, while preserving wa’s safety and schema-stability guarantees.

## Endpoints (v0)
- `GET /health`
- `GET /panes`
- `GET /events?unhandled=1&pane_id=...&limit=...`
- `GET /search?q=...&pane_id=...&since=...&limit=...`

## Design constraints
- Reuse the same query/model code as CLI/robot.
  - The web server should not reimplement data access.
- Responses are JSON with stable schemas and stable ordering.
- Apply the same redaction pipeline as robot/audit/export.

## Pagination & limits
- All list endpoints must be bounded.
  - enforce default and max `limit`
  - provide stable ordering (e.g., newest-first for events)

## Error handling
- Return structured JSON errors with stable error codes.
- Never leak internal DB paths or sensitive config in errors.

## Testing
- Covered by the web server tests bead (`wa-nu4.3.6.4`):
  - schema parseability
  - redaction
  - bind defaults

## Acceptance Criteria
- Endpoints return data when watcher is running.
- Responses remain schema-stable and redacted.
- List endpoints enforce sane defaults and max limits.
